task: The Java method Loyalty.points(spend, member) awards too many points for ordinary repeat purchases. The calculation is spread across Loyalty, Tier and Promo. Exactly one line adds a bonus under a condition that is true for almost every purchase (it should only apply in a special case). Find it. Answer with: BUG LINE <n>, the code, and REACHED-WHEN <condition>.
bug: In `Promo.apply`, `points = points + 5;` runs whenever `spend > 0` — so the "first purchase" bonus is added on every purchase, doubling points for repeat buyers. The call chain Loyalty.points → tier → promo spreads it across 3 files.
